title: Justicia leonardii
intro: Justicia leonardii, also known as Banaba, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Asia, belonging to the family Acanthaceae. The extracts and leaves of Justicia leonardii have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including diabetes and obesity, due to its potential health benefits such as lowering blood sugar levels and regulating lipid profiles. The bioactive constituents of Justicia leonardii include corosolic acid, which is a triterpene glycoside responsible for its anti-diabetic properties, as well as other compounds like 6-hydroxy-wighteic acid and other flavonoids. Justicia leonardii is commonly used in herbal preparations such as capsules, tablets, and teas, as well as in extracts and powders that are marketed as dietary supplements to support blood sugar control and weight management.
taxonomy: Justicia leonardii is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It is native to tropical Africa and is commonly known as Leonard's Justicia.
